title: Remove unused code and data structures
body:
No change in functionality.

I might be missing something here, but it appears to be either unfinished or abandoned.

Fixes: bbd085ad1e ("Podman Pod Create --cpus and --cpuset-cpus flags", PR #10716)
Fixes: 2d86051893 ("Pod Device-Read-BPS support", PR #11686)

Cc @cdoern 

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?


```release-note
NONE
```

comments.body: Can't test it further unless someone will add `No New Tests` label.
@kolyshkin this is passing tests.  Time to drop the "Draft" bit?
@mheon were any of these attributes planned for pods and just didnt get implement ?

`ThrottleReadBpsDevice` is the only one that might be relevant, because it differs from what's in the actual container resource config; the others, I don't see a reason for them to exist when the resource config duplicates them.
(But I question how much device-based limits make sense when containers in a pod aren't sharing `/dev/`?)

Thanks @kolyshkin , I honestly don't recall the order of operations here. It's possible I had some plans for pod resource limits and these were a pre-cursor and I didn't get to the follow up PRs? But yeah, I agree with what @mheon said, beyond that one resource limit we should remove the duplication unless there is a functional reason to keep them. 

Perhaps my thinking was applying these to the infra-container or propagating the resource limit to each container created in the pod? unsure.
happy to take another look or finish up this work if there is a need :)

> `ThrottleReadBpsDevice` is the only one that might be relevant, because it differs from what's in the actual container resource config; the others, I don't see a reason for them to exist when the resource config duplicates them.

From what I see, `ThrottleReadBpsDevice` member of `struct PodResourceConfig` is not used or assigned anywhere. This field is also exists in the same structure as `PodResourceConfig.ResourceLimits.BlockIO.ThrottleReadBpsDevice`. It makes no sense to have two values for `ThrottleReadBpsDevice` in `struct PodResourceConfig`.

Overall, to my best understanding, `struct PodResourceConfig` is a per-pod structure, which is there so we can have per-pod values different from that of containers'.

So, should I still keep it?

`PodResourceConfig` (if I remember correctly) was intended to apply resource limits to an entire pod unless a container has resource limits that override (or something like that)

Having this seems useful to me such that containers in a pod have the same resource configurations/limits. 

I would ensure each of the resources are properly propagated to containers added to a pod, including infra. I think this was my intention when creating this feature back during my internship

@mheon @baude OK guess this should be merged as is, and if there's anything that needs to be added in the future, it should be added. It does not make sense to keep fields that are not used anywhere in the code.
